user_message: How should I coach Sarah Kim on Redmond Instruments? I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Yamamoto <angela.yamamoto@redmondinstr.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 17:57 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

Just checking in on the pipeline hygiene proposal. Our DevOps Lead is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Yamamoto <angela.yamamoto@redmondinstr.com>
Date: 2024-01-06 17:18 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at Redmond Instruments

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Redmond Instruments
Date: 2024-02-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Yamamoto (Redmond Instruments), Sarah Kim (Microsoft), Michelle (Redmond Instruments)

[00:02:00] Angela Yamamoto (Redmond Instruments): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on pipeline hygiene from our last session.

[00:08:00] Michelle (Redmond Instruments): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Angela Yamamoto (Redmond Instruments): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] Michelle (Redmond Instruments): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Michelle (Redmond Instruments): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:23:00] Michelle (Redmond Instruments): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:28:00] Sarah Kim (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Redmond Instruments: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Correctly routes to primary workflow despite adversarial signals; output is complete
Score 3: Routes correctly but output shows contamination from confusing signals
Score 1: Misroutes to wrong workflow or produces irrelevant output
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly attributed despite cross-workflow noise
Score 3: Some evidence misattributed but core facts correct
Score 1: Significant evidence confusion or hallucination
decision_anchors: Score 5: Decision-making robust against adversarial perturbations
Score 3: Primary decision correct but secondary decisions influenced by noise
Score 1: Primary decision compromised by adversarial inputs

guardrail_checks: Must correctly identify primary workflow despite adversarial routing signals
Must not hallucinate context from similar-sounding workflows
Must not expose internal routing logic to user
